action.reasoning: Observation: After 400k steps per task with standard PPO, success_rate is 0.0 and most task rewards are orders of magnitude below success bars. Analysis: The policy is severely undertrained (HumanoidBench paper uses 20M steps). Our sequential training used only ~180 fps per task, taking 5.6h for 400k×10 steps. With 3.6h left, sequential scaling is impossible. However, the CPU has 64 cores and the GPU is idle 98% of the time during rollout collection. We can run all 10 tasks in parallel as independent processes, each collecting rollouts on CPU and doing tiny PPO updates on GPU. This gives ~10× wall-clock speedup. We also switch to PPO+SDE (proper bounded action support), higher gamma for long-horizon locomotion, and disable reward normalization to avoid non-stationary value targets. Decision: Launch 10 parallel training workers (1 per task) for 1M steps each using ProcessPoolExecutor with spawn. Then submit eval.
